articles.title: SHIP THIRD-PARTY INTEGRATIONS WITHOUT REBUILDING OAUTH
articles.summary: Every integration comes with the same overhead: OAuth flows, token storage, refresh logic, and provider-specific quirks. It's tedious, time-consuming, and pulls your team away from core product work.

articles.title: Let your team focus on product. Use WorkOS Pipes →
SOFTWARE IS DEAD…AGAIN!
articles.summary: Cloud software valuations have cratered to 10-year lows, with the median NTM revenue multiple hitting 4.1x. Investor confidence in the SaaS business model is fracturing as AI disruption risks threaten the retention rates and terminal value assumptions that powered the last decade of software investing. The path back requires companies to prove stable retention over multiple quarters.

articles.title: LEADERS, GAINERS, AND UNEXPECTED WINNERS IN THE ENTERPRISE AI ARMS RACE
articles.summary: a16z surveyed 100 Global 2000 CIOs and found OpenAI still leads enterprise AI with 78% production usage, but Anthropic posted the largest share gain at +25% and now sits at 44% penetration. The surprise winner in enterprise apps is still Microsoft, not the startups dominating tech Twitter. Average enterprise AI spend rose from $4.5M to $7M and is expected to hit $11.6M this year.

articles.title: THE BEST AI STARTUPS DO THE TRAINING FOR YOU. IT'S ON YOU TO MAKE THE AGENT AWESOME — NOT YOUR CUSTOMERS
articles.summary: The old model was to sell the software and let the customer figure it out. Businesses now need to do the work to make their AI great before they hand over the keys. AI agents will fail if improperly contained. It is up to the AI vendor to make it work. Customers shouldn't have to get agents to work.

articles.title: WHAT TAM SHOULD ACTUALLY TELL YOU
articles.summary: Stop calculating “potential customers × potential price” and start with what the world currently spends solving your problem. That's your actual market today. Everything beyond that isn't market size, it's a thesis about growth. Most founders are stacking 3-5 speculative theses without realizing it, and since each compounds risk exponentially (30% × 30% × 30%), understanding this can dramatically improve how you allocate time and opportunity cost.

articles.title: HOW PRODUCT DISCOVERY CHANGES WITH AI
articles.summary: AI has collapsed the cost and time of building and testing products, which means feasibility, viability, and usability are no longer the hard parts: desirability is. The critical bottleneck in product discovery is now understanding what customers actually want. Teams need to shift investment toward deeper customer insight and research to answer the fundamental question before building anything.

Spreadsheets weren't built for ownership at scale. Carta

articles.summary: Robynn is an AI marketing platform that helps founders create branded content.

articles.title: KOMBAI
articles.summary: AI agent for real frontend tasks: build UIs, refactor, and integrate.

articles.title: LOYALTY IS DEAD IN TECH
articles.summary: Big Tech has figured out how to acqui-hire without the acquisition. License the IP, hire who you want, leave the rest behind. No regulatory review, no shared outcomes. Microsoft did it with Inflection, Amazon with Adept, and Google with Character AI. The unwritten oath between founders, employees, and investors is dead. When picking your next company, ask your VC who else they've backed in your category and what happened to employees at the founder's last company.

articles.title: THE ORIGINS OF GENERATIONAL COMPANIES
articles.summary: Microsoft, Oracle, NVIDIA, and Starbucks all started by selling something to a tiny, often invisible market before stumbling into bigger opportunities. The playbook isn't “envision a radically different future and build it,” it's figure out a real need by selling, learn by iterating on what doesn't work, avoid trying to predict the future or convince markets, and stay in the game long enough to catch a demand wave when it hits.

articles.title: RETHINKING SLACK
articles.summary: Slack faced issues with feature bloat and complexity by late 2019, as the product accumulated many additions without a cohesive structure.

articles.title: FOLLOW YOUR PASSION, BUT NOT LIKE THAT
articles.summary: Chase your passion with a blend of curiosity and practicality rather than as a blind directive.

articles.title: REVISITING CLARITY OF THOUGHT
articles.summary: Clarity of thought stands as a key differentiator for successful founders, influencing all aspects from hiring to sales.

articles.title: B2CC - CLAUDE CODE IS YOUR CUSTOMER
articles.summary: We are now shifting into an 'agents as customers' era - companies need to be thinking about how to become the vendor that AI agents prefer.
